content.summary: Greenfield Municipal Council — previous order of 100 solar street lights completed successfully in 2023. Delivery was 3 days late due to customs delay. Client was understanding but noted timeline concerns. Payment was received net-60 as agreed.
content.key_lesson: Customs clearance for government orders may add 3-5 days to delivery timeline. Factor into scheduling for repeat orders.

content.summary: SolarTech Manufacturing — consistently reliable for large orders. 200 panel order in 2024 delivered on time. Quality inspection passed with 98.7% pass rate. Communication responsive.
content.key_lesson: Preferred for quantity orders over 100 units. Lead time of 10 days is reliable but tight for urgent requests.

content.summary: Organizational policy requires minimum 15% gross margin on all orders. Exceptions require Operations Manager approval and documented justification.
AfriEnergy Components — regional supplier in Africa. Faster lead times (5 days) but higher unit cost ($210). Good track record on small-to-medium orders. Net-30 payment terms.
content.key_lesson: Best option when delivery speed is priority and order is under 200 units. Higher margin impact.

content.summary: Previous decision to use international supplier (SolarTech) involved currency risk. USD-denominated contract with 30% deposit. Naira fluctuation caused 4% margin erosion. Finance recommended hedging for future international orders.
content.key_lesson: International supplier contracts should include currency risk assessment and consider Naira hedging.

content.summary: RenPower Africa — repeat client with good relationship. Previous order of 200 panels was upsold from 150. Client appreciates volume discounts and fast communication.
content.key_lesson: RenPower responds well to proactive suggestions. Can potentially upsell value-added services.
